title: An ultra performance liquid chromatography coupled with high resolution mass spectrometry method for the screening of cianotoxins content in drinking water samples
author: di pofi, Favero, et al.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: This work describes an advanced analytical method for simultaneous determination of 21 cyanotoxins of four different class, including 12 Microcystins (MC), 2 Cyanopeptolins(CYP),2 Anabaenopeptins (ANAB) and 5 Microginins (MICRO).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: For the analysis of total Cyanotoxins content, 250 mL of water sample were freezed, filtered, spiked with Nodularin (used as internal standard) at 1 µg/L and then extracted using Carbograph 4 SPE cartridge.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: For the sample extraction a previous method [1] was updated adding Cyanopeptolins, Anabeanenopeptins and Microginins as analytes and the extraction efficiency was optimised for all the selected cyanotoxins.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: All cyanotoxins standards were purchased by Alexis® Biochemicals (La Jolla, 96 CA, USA) while the reagents used were RS grade and the water was obtained from a Milli-Q system (Millipore Bedford, MA, USA).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The instruments used in this work were:•Acquity UPLC system (Waters Corp., Milford, MA, USA)•Acquity UPLC BEH C18 column (2,1 mm ID × 100 mm, 1,7 µm, Waters Corp., Milford, MA, USA)•XEVO G2S Q-TOF (Waters Corp., Milford, MA, USA)

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The presence and the quantization of the selected analytes were obtained used:•a standard solution containing 0.1 μg/L of all the analytes and 1 μg/L of Nodularin used as internal standard (IS), prepared in 70:30 H2O: ACN 10 mM formic acid without any extraction was used•four water samples spiked at four different concentration levels (0.1, 0.5, 1.0 and 2.0 μg/L) to prepare the calibration curve for each analytes

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: All the condition applied to obtain the best chromatographic separation for all the analytes was reported in Table 1.Table 1Phase A: water 10 mM formic acid; Phase B: acetonitrile 10 mM formic acid; %As: % phase A start time; %Af: % phase A end time.Table 1:Start time → end time (min)Column temp (°C)Flux (µl/min)%As→ %Af0 → 44045090 →704 → 84045070 → 308 → 104045030 → 010 → 12404500 → 012 → 13404500 → 9013 → 164045090 → 90

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Mass Spectrometry analyses were operated in:•sensitivity mode and positive ionization•scan time of 0.5 s•source temperature of 130°C and desolvation temperature of 500°C•desolvation gas flow of 1000.0 L/Hr

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The lock mass signal was used to correct automatically the analytes mass detected during the experiment.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: It is necessary the presence of molecular and fragment ion, at the same retention time (reported in Table 2), to be sure to detect the analites.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The method has proven to be robust, precise and accurate with recovery percentages above 85% and with relative standard deviations ≤16%, fitting for the intended purposes at the concentrations of interest.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: No matrix effect ware observed for all the selected analytes.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The LODs were in the range 0.002 to 0.047 µg/L, as reported in Table 2, and all the obtained values at least 20-fold lower than the guideline value proposed by WHO for drinking water (1.0 µg/L for microcystin-LR). [2]

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The limit of quantitation (LOQ) could be calculated by multiplying for 3 the LODs values.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The described method allows to detect and quantify the presence of 21 cyanotoxins of different classes in water samples for human consumption simultaneously, in short time of analysis (16 minutes) with a good resolution for all the analytes.
